rulings.Case Title: SUZANNE WALTERICH VS. ANNA'S LINENS INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 1, 2011, line 4. DEFENDANT ANNA'S LINENS INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Defendant's objections to declaration of plaintiff are overruled. There is a triable issue of material fact as to whether defendant was aware of the aggressive solicitations at the landing of defendant's store and if this solicitation created a foreseeable risk of harm to its patrons. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: EVA HALL VS. CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 1, 2011, line 7. DEFENDANT DENNIS SHEN'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant's demurrer to the 2nd cause of action (concealment) is overruled. Plaintiff has pled facts sufficient to state a claim. However, to the extent that the cause of action is asserted because medical records were not passed to Stanford or other 3rd parties, plaintiff lacks standing to sue on those other entities' behalf. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: EVA HALL VS. CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 1, 2011, line 7. DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant's demurrer is overruled. Plaintiff states facts sufficient to state her claims for negligence and concealment. Defendant's statute of limitations was raised in its reply and is improper. Nonetheless, plaintff has pled factual allegations that are sufficient to toll the statute of limitations. Defendant must file an answer within 10 days. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: EVA HALL VS. CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER et al

rulings.Case Title: THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA, BY AND VS. INVESTCO MANAGEMENT & DEVELOPMENT LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 1, 2011, line 9. PLAINTIFF THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S Motion For Preliminary Injunction is granted. Plaintiff and defendant agree that plaintiff's claim is likely to prevail on the merits. Moreover, plaintiff has shown that the balance of harm tips in favor of plaintiff and those plaintiff seeks to protect through this order (defendant's investors). =(302/LMG)
